---
title: "Exercise 05 — Testing Apptainer/Singularity containers"
format: html
toc: true
---

::: {.callout-note}
🇫🇷 [Version française](../../../exercises/05_testing_containers/README.qmd)
:::

## Objective

Understand why a container alone is not enough to guarantee reproducibility,
and learn how to test a container **independently** of the pipeline, then **integrated** into a Nextflow process.

---

## Why test containers?

A versioned container is a good foundation, but it is not sufficient.

::: {.callout-warning}
**An untested container is not a guarantee.**

The image may start without the tool being accessible, the version being correct,
or the Nextflow process actually working inside it.
:::

Two levels of testing must be distinguished:

| Level | What is verified | Tool |
|-------|-----------------|------|
| **Container alone** | The image starts, the tool exists, the version is correct | `bats` + `apptainer exec` |
| **Containerised Nextflow process** | Nextflow actually uses the container, the process completes, outputs are correct | `nf-test --profile apptainer` |

---

## Common risks to avoid

| Bad practice | Risk |
|-------------|------|
| `latest` tag | The image changes without warning |
| Network pull at a critical moment | Timeout or unexpected version in production |
| Testing with Docker, deploying with Apptainer | Different behaviours (bind mounts, permissions) |
| Custom image without a versioned `.def` | Cannot be rebuilt identically |
| No `--version` test | The installed version is unknown |
| Hardcoded writes to `/home` or `/tmp` | Non-reproducible path depending on the environment |
| Multi-tool container without justification | Difficult to update, larger attack surface |

---

## Structure of this exercise

```
05_testing_containers/
├── containers/
│   ├── fastqc.def                  # Apptainer definition (reproducible)
│   └── README.md                   # How to obtain the image
└── tests/
    ├── container_fastqc.bats       # Tests for the container alone (bats)
    └── fastqc_container.nf.test    # Test for the containerised Nextflow process (nf-test)
```

---

## Part 1 — Testing the container alone

### Obtaining the image

```bash
cd training/exercises/05_testing_containers/containers

# Option rapide : pull depuis BioContainers
apptainer pull fastqc_0.12.1.sif \
  docker://biocontainers/fastqc:0.12.1--hdfd78af_0

# Option reproductible : build depuis le fichier de définition
apptainer build fastqc_0.12.1.sif fastqc.def
```

### bats exercise

Complete the tests in `tests/container_fastqc.bats`:

```bash
@test "fastqc --version rapporte la version attendue" {
    # TODO : exécuter fastqc --version dans le container
    # TODO : vérifier le status et le contenu de la sortie
}

@test "fastqc est accessible sans monter /home" {
    # TODO : utiliser --no-home
    # TODO : vérifier que l'outil fonctionne quand même
}
```

### Running the tests

```bash
bats training/exercises/05_testing_containers/tests/container_fastqc.bats
```

::: {.callout-warning}
**SKIPPED ≠ PASSED.**

If the first test (`infrastructure — apptainer installé et image SIF présente`) is SKIPPED,
all subsequent tests will also be skipped. This means **no container has been verified**.
Tests must be run on an HPC node with Apptainer installed and the SIF image present.
:::

---

## Part 2 — Testing the containerised Nextflow process

### How Nextflow uses the container

The `FASTQC` process declares its container:

```groovy
process FASTQC {
    container 'biocontainers/fastqc:0.12.1--hdfd78af_0'
    ...
}
```

This container is ignored without a dedicated profile.
It is activated with `-profile apptainer` or `-profile docker`.

### nf-test exercise

Complete `tests/fastqc_container.nf.test` to verify:

- that the process succeeds with the apptainer profile
- that the outputs exist
- that at least one business value is correct

### Running with Apptainer

```bash
cd training/exercises/05_testing_containers/tests

# Avec Apptainer (recommandé en HPC)
nf-test test fastqc_container.nf.test --profile apptainer

# Sans container (simulation, pour développement rapide)
nf-test test fastqc_container.nf.test
```

---

## Expected result

```bash
# Tests bats container
✓ SIF image file exists
✓ fastqc --version exits cleanly and reports expected version
✓ which fastqc returns a path inside /usr or /opt
✓ fastqc is accessible without mounting /home
✓ container is writable only in expected locations
✓ apptainer exec fails clearly if image is missing

# nf-test
✔ reads valides → HTML et stats produits dans le container
✔ fichier vide → process échoue dans le container
```

## Solution

→ See [solution.qmd](solution.qmd)
